Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a process tree that is underfitting The current model has a sequence create req approve req parallel block of create po receive goods receive invoice match invoice pay invoice close case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The problem is that the parallel block allows the activities inside to occur in any order and even concurrently

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: However in a typical Procure to Pay process there is a logical order 1 Create Purchase Requisition 2 Approve Purchase Requisition 3 Create Purchase Order 4 Receive Goods 5 Receive Invoice 6 Match Invoice to PO 7 Pay Invoice 8 Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The purchase order step 3 must be created after the requisition is approved

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The goods receipt step 4 and invoice receipt step 5 can happen in any order or concurrently

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: Sometimes matching can be done even before goods receipt two way match or after three way match

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Given the sequence in the problem statement we are told to follow the sequence 3 4 5 6

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: We have two options Option 1 Enforce the exact sequence as listed 3 4 5 6 7 8 Option 2 Allow some flexibility if it is domain appropriate

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Given the problem statement it says the process is intended to follow the standard logic 1 8 in order So we should enforce the sequence as Sequence Create Purchase Requisition Approve Purchase Requisition Create Purchase Order Receive Goods Receive Invoice Match Invoice to PO Pay Invoice Close Case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The current model has a parallel block for steps 3 7 We are to fix the model to only allow the normal order

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Therefore the corrected process tree should be root equals ProcessTree operator equals Operator SEQUENCE children equals create req approve req create po receive goods receive invoice match invoice pay invoice close case

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: We can keep the structure of having a sequence for the first two then a sequence for the next five then the last one But that would be the same as a flat sequence

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The intention is to keep the same structure that is the root is a sequence with four elements and the third element is now a sequence instead of a parallel

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Corrected structure Instead of a parallel block we have a sequence for the steps after approval and before closing sequence block equals ProcessTree operator equals Operator SEQUENCE children equals create po receive goods receive invoice match invoice pay invoice root equals ProcessTree operator equals Operator SEQUENCE children equals create req approve req sequence block close case

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: In our corrected model the sequence is enforced create po receive goods receive invoice match invoice pay invoice close case So that unwanted behavior is prevented

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The updated process tree replaces the parallel block which allowed any order and concurrency with a sequence block that enforces the exact order Create Purchase Order Receive Goods Receive Invoice Match Invoice to PO Pay Invoice

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This sequential structure prevents any deviation from the intended order such as paying an invoice before receiving goods or matching an invoice before receiving it
